name: SharonAI Holdings Inc.
business: SharonAI Holdings Inc. is an Australian neocloud operator that provides enterprise, government, and research organizations with sovereign, low-latency access to advanced accelerated computing hardware, including NVIDIA GPUs. It offers GPU-as-a-Service, AI Studio (PaaS), and cloud storage solutions for AI and HPC workloads.

exposures.sentence: SharonAI Holdings, Inc. is an Australian neocloud operator, purpose-built to power the next generation of artificial intelligence ( AI ) and high-performance computing ( HPC ).

Rather than incurring large capital expenditure and multi-year investment in building its own data centers, the Company deploys its infrastructure directly into the facilities of data center partners such as NEXTDC, under long-term contractual arrangements.

We are substantially dependent on NVIDIA Corporation as a supplier of graphics processing units ( GPUs ), and any disruption in our ability to obtain NVIDIA GPUs could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, and results of operations.

catalysts.event: B200 GPU cluster deployment coming online
catalysts.sentence: The balance of this cluster is expected to consist of NVIDIA B200 GPUs expected to come online in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: B300 GPU cluster deployment coming online
catalysts.sentence: We are also deploying a 1,024-unit NVIDIA B300 GPU cluster which will be located at NEXTDC’s S3 data center and expected to be coming online in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Potential investment from Digital Alpha of up to $200 million and strategic technology partnership with Cisco
catalysts.sentence: On January 19, 2026, we announced a potential investment from Digital Alpha of up to $200 million and a strategic technology partnership with Cisco, subject to execution of definitive documentation.

catalysts.event: Debt facility of up to US$500 million from USD.AI
catalysts.sentence: On January 22, 2026, we announced that USD.AI had approved a debt facility of up to US$500 million for SharonAI, a subsidiary of the Company, subject to execution of definitive documentation.
